sections.label: Section 1129
sections.original: εἶτʼ αὐτὸ δηλοῖ τοὔργον οἷ τείνειν χρεών .
sections.translation: And then the deed itself shows us where we must exert ourselves .

sections.label: Section 1130
sections.original: Ἑλένην φονεύειν · μανθάνω τὸ σύμβολον .
sections.translation: To kill Helen ; I understand that watchword .

sections.label: Section 1131
sections.original: ἔγνως · ἄκουσον δʼ ὡς καλῶς βουλεύομαι .
sections.translation: You have it ; now hear how sound my scheme is . If we drew the sword upon a woman of greater chastity , the murder would be infamous ; but , as it is , she will be punished for the sake of all Hellas ,

sections.label: Section 1135
sections.original: ὧν πατέρας ἔκτεινʼ , ὧν δʼ ἀπώλεσεν τέκνα ,
sections.translation: whose fathers she slew , whose children she destroyed , and made widows out of brides . There will be shouts of joy , and they will kindle the altars of the gods , invoking on our heads many blessings , because we shed a wicked woman ’ s blood .

sections.label: Section 1140
sections.original: ὁ μητροφόντης δʼ οὐ καλῇ ταύτην κτανών ,
sections.translation: After killing her , you will not be called the matricide , but , resigning that title , you will succeed to a better , and be called the slayer of Helen the murderess . It can never , never be right that Menelaus should prosper , and your father , your sister and you should die ,

sections.label: Section 1145
sections.original: μητέρα τε — ἐῶ τοῦτʼ · οὐ γὰρ εὐπρεπὲς λέγειν —
sections.translation: and your mother — but I pass that by , for it is not seemly to mention it — and for him to possess your home , though it was by Agamemnon ’ s prowess that he recovered his bride . May I die , if we do not draw our swords upon her ! But if we do not accomplish Helen ’ s death ,

sections.label: Section 1150
sections.original: πρήσαντες οἴκους τούσδε κατθανούμεθα .
sections.translation: we will set fire to the house and die . For we will not fail to achieve one distinction , an honorable death or an honorable escape .

sections.label: Section 1153
sections.original: πάσαις γυναιξὶν ἀξία στυγεῖν ἔφυ
sections.translation: The daughter of Tyndareus , who has brought shame on her sex , has justly earned the hatred of every woman .

sections.label: Section 1155
sections.original: φεῦ ·
sections.translation: Ah ! there is nothing better than a trusty friend , neither wealth nor monarchy ; a crowd of people is of no account in exchange for a noble friend . You were the one who devised the vengeance against Aegisthus , and stood by me in danger ,

sections.label: Section 1160
sections.original: νῦν τʼ αὖ δίδως μοι πολεμίων τιμωρίαν
sections.translation: and now again you are offering me a means to punish my foes and do not stand aside — but I will cease praising you , for there is something wearisome even in being praised to excess . Now since in any case I must breathe my last , I want to do something to my enemies before my death ,

sections.label: Section 1165
sections.original: ἵνʼ ἀνταναλώσω μὲν οἵ με προύδοσαν ,
sections.translation: so that I may requite with ruin those who betrayed me , and so that those who made me suffer may grieve . Yes ! I am the son of Agamemnon , who was considered worthy to rule Hellas , no tyrant but yet god-like in power ; I will not disgrace him

sections.label: Section 1170
sections.original: δοῦλον παρασχὼν θάνατον , ἀλλʼ ἐλευθέρως
sections.translation: by submitting to die like a slave ; my last breath shall be free and I will take vengeance on Menelaus . For if we could secure one object , we would be lucky , if a means of safety should unexpectedly come our way from somewhere , and we should be the slayers , not the slain ; this is what I pray for .

sections.label: Section 1175
sections.original: ὃ βούλομαι γάρ , ἡδὺ καὶ διὰ στόμα
sections.translation: This wish of mine is a pleasant dream to cheer the heart , without cost , by means of the mouth ’ s winged words .

sections.label: Section 1177
sections.original: ἐγώ , κασίγνητʼ , αὐτὸ τοῦτʼ ἔχειν δοκῶ ,
sections.translation: I think I have it , brother , a means of safety for you , and for him and thirdly for myself .

sections.label: Section 1179
sections.original: θεοῦ λέγεις πρόνοιαν . ἀλλὰ ποῦ τόδε ;
sections.translation: You mean divine providence . But why do I say that ?

sections.label: Section 1180
sections.original: ἐπεὶ τὸ συνετόν γʼ οἶδα σῇ ψυχῇ παρόν .
sections.translation: Since I know the natural shrewdness of your heart .

sections.label: Section 1181
sections.original: ἄκουε δή νυν · καὶ σὺ δεῦρο νοῦν ἔχε .
sections.translation: Listen to me now ; and you ( to Pylades ) pay attention also .

sections.label: Section 1182
sections.original: λέγʼ · ὡς τὸ μέλλειν ἀγάθʼ ἔχει τινʼ ἡδονήν .
sections.translation: Speak ; the prospect of good news holds a certain pleasure .

sections.label: Section 1183
sections.original: Ἑλένης κάτοισθα θυγατέρʼ ; εἰδότʼ ἠρόμην .
sections.translation: You know Helen ’ s daughter ? Of course you do .

sections.label: Section 1184
sections.original: οἶδʼ , ἣν ἔθρεψεν Ἑρμιόνην μήτηρ ἐμή .
sections.translation: I know her , Hermione , whom my mother reared .

sections.label: Section 1185
sections.original: αὕτη βέβηκε πρὸς Κλυταιμήστρας τάφον .
sections.translation: She has gone to Clytemnestra ’ s tomb .

sections.label: Section 1186
sections.original: τί χρῆμα δράσουσʼ ; ὑποτίθης τίνʼ ἐλπίδα ;
sections.translation: To do what ? What hope are you hinting at ?

sections.label: Section 1187
sections.original: χοὰς κατασπείσουσʼ ὑπὲρ μητρὸς τάφῳ .
sections.translation: She was going to pour a libation over the tomb of our mother .

sections.label: Section 1188
sections.original: καὶ δὴ τί μοι τοῦτʼ εἶπας ἐς σωτηρίαν ;
sections.translation: Well , how does what you have said lead to our safety ?

sections.label: Section 1189
sections.original: ξυλλάβεθʼ ὅμηρον τήνδʼ , ὅταν στείχῃ πάλιν .
sections.translation: Seize her as a hostage on her way back .

sections.label: Section 1190
sections.original: τίνος τόδʼ εἶπας φάρμακον τρισσοῖς φίλοις ;
sections.translation: What good can your suggested remedy do us three friends ?

sections.label: Section 1191
sections.original: Ἑλένης θανούσης , ἤν τι Μενέλεώς σε δρᾷ
sections.translation: If , after Helen ’ s slaughter , Menelaus tries to do anything to you or to Pylades and me — for this bond of friendship is wholly one — say that you will kill Hermione ; you must draw your sword and hold it to the maiden ’ s throat .

sections.label: Section 1195
sections.original: κἂν μέν σε σῴζῃ μὴ θανεῖν χρῄζων κόρην
sections.translation: If Menelaus , when he sees Helen fallen in her blood , tries to save you to insure the girl ’ s life , allow him to take his daughter to his arms ; but if he makes no effort to curb the angry outburst and leaves you to die , then cut the maiden ’ s throat .

sections.label: Section 1200
sections.original: καί νιν δοκῶ , τὸ πρῶτον ἢν πολὺς παρῇ ,
sections.translation: And I think if he puts in a mighty appearance at first , he will calm down in time ; for he is not bold or brave by nature . That is my line of defense for our safety . My speech is over .

sections.label: Section 1204
sections.original: ὦ τὰς φρένας μὲν ἄρσενας κεκτημένη ,
sections.translation: O you that have the spirit of a man ,

sections.label: Section 1205
sections.original: τὸ σῶμα δʼ ἐν γυναιξὶ θηλείαις πρέπον ,
sections.translation: though your body shows you to be a woman , how far more worthy you are to live than to die ! Pylades , you will lose such a woman to your sorrow , or if you live , you will have a blessed marriage .

sections.label: Section 1209
sections.original: εἰ γὰρ γένοιτο , Φωκέων δʼ ἔλθοι πόλιν
sections.translation: Then may it be so , and may she come to the city of Phocis

sections.label: Section 1210
sections.original: καλοῖσιν ὑμεναίοισιν ἀξιουμένη .
sections.translation: with all the honors of a happy wedding .

sections.label: Section 1211
sections.original: ἥξει δʼ ἐς οἴκους Ἑρμιόνη τίνος χρόνου ;
sections.translation: How soon will Hermione return to the palace ? All the rest was very well said , if we succeed in catching this impious father ’ s cub .

sections.label: Section 1214
sections.original: καὶ δὴ πέλας νιν δωμάτων εἶναι δοκῶ ·
sections.translation: Well , I expect she is near the house already ,

sections.label: Section 1215
sections.original: τοῦ γὰρ χρόνου τὸ μῆκος αὐτὸ συντρέχει .
sections.translation: for the length of time agrees exactly .

sections.label: Section 1216
sections.original: καλῶς · σὺ μέν νυν , σύγγονʼ Ἠλέκτρα , δόμων
sections.translation: Good ; you , Electra , my sister , stay before the palace and await the maiden ’ s approach ; keep watch in case any one , whether an ally or my father ’ s brother , forestalls us by his entry before the murder is complete ;

sections.label: Section 1220
sections.original: ἐλθὼν ἐς οἴκους φθῇ , γέγωνέ τʼ ἐς δόμους ,
sections.translation: and then make a signal to the house , either by beating on a panel of the door or calling to us within .

sections.label: Section 1222
sections.original: ἡμεῖς δʼ ἔσω στείχοντες ἐπὶ τὸν ἔσχατον
sections.translation: Let us enter now and arm ourselves with swords for the final struggle , Pylades , for you share the labor with me .

sections.label: Section 1225
sections.original: ὦ δῶμα ναίων νυκτὸς ὀρφναίας πάτερ ,
sections.translation: O father , in your home of gloomy night , your son Orestes calls you to come to the rescue of the destitute . It is on your account I am wrongfully suffering , and it is by your brother that I have been betrayed for doing right ; it is his wife I wish to take

sections.label: Section 1230
sections.original: κτεῖναι · σὺ δʼ ἡμῖν τοῦδε συλλήπτωρ γενοῦ .
sections.translation: and kill ; you be our accomplice for this deed .

sections.label: Section 1231
sections.original: ὦ πάτερ , ἱκοῦ δῆτʼ , εἰ κλύεις ἔσω χθονὸς
sections.translation: Oh father , come ! if within the ground you hear the cry of your children , who are dying for your sake .

sections.label: Section 1233
sections.original: ὦ συγγένεια πατρὸς ἐμοῦ , κἀμὰς λιτάς ,
sections.translation: O kinsman of my father , Agamemnon , hear my prayers also ; save your children .

sections.label: Section 1235
sections.original: ἔκτεινα μητέρα —
sections.translation: I killed my mother —

sections.label: Section 1235b
sections.original: ἡψάμην δʼ ἐγὼ ξίφους —
sections.translation: I held the sword —

sections.label: Section 1236
sections.original: ἐγὼ δʼ ἐπεβούλευσα κἀπέλυσʼ ὄκνου —
sections.translation: I . . . set them free from fear —

sections.label: Section 1237
sections.original: σοί , πάτερ , ἀρήγων .
sections.translation: To aid you , father .

sections.label: Section 1237b
sections.original: οὐδʼ ἐγὼ προύδωκά σε .
sections.translation: Nor did I betray you .

sections.label: Section 1238
sections.original: οὔκουν ὀνείδη τάδε κλύων ῥύσῃ τέκνα ;
sections.translation: Will you not hear these reproaches and rescue your children ?

sections.label: Section 1239
sections.original: δακρύοις κατασπένδω σε .
sections.translation: With tears I pour you a libation .

sections.label: Section 1239b
sections.original: ἐγὼ δʼ οἴκτοισί γε .
sections.translation: And I with laments .

sections.label: Section 1240
sections.original: παύσασθε , καὶ πρὸς ἔργον ἐξορμώμεθα .
sections.translation: Cease , and let us set about our business . If prayers really do pierce the ground , he hears . O Zeus , god of my fathers , and holy Justice , give success to him and me and her ; for there is one struggle for three friends , and one penalty ,

sections.label: Section 1245
sections.original: ἢ ζῆν ἅπασιν ἢ — θανεῖν ὀφείλεται .
sections.translation: for all to live or — pay death ’ s account . Exeunt Orestes and Pylades .

sections.label: Section 1.1246
sections.original: Μυκηνίδες ὦ φίλαι ,
sections.translation: My dear friends of Mycenae , of foremost rank in Argos , the home of the Pelasgians .

sections.label: Section 1.1248
sections.original: τίνα θροεῖς αὐδάν , πότνια ; παραμένει
sections.translation: What are you saying to us , mistress ?

sections.label: Section 1.1250
sections.original: γὰρ ἔτι σοι τόδʼ ἐν Δαναϊδῶν πόλει .
sections.translation: For this honored name is still left for you in the Danaid town .

sections.label: Section 1.1251
sections.original: στῆθʼ αἳ μὲν ὑμῶν τόνδʼ ἁμαξήρη τρίβον ,
sections.translation: Station yourselves , some here along the high road , others there on some other path , to watch the house .

sections.label: Section 1.1253
sections.original: τί δέ με τόδε χρέος ἀπύεις ;
sections.translation: But why do you call me to this service ? Tell me , my dear .

sections.label: Section 1.1255
sections.original: φόβος ἔχει με μή τις ἐπὶ δώμασι
sections.translation: I am afraid that some one , who is stationed at the house for slaughter , may find trouble upon trouble .

sections.label: Section 1.1258
sections.original: χωρεῖτʼ , ἐπειγώμεσθʼ · ἐγὼ μὲν οὖν τρίβον
sections.translation: Let us make haste and go on ; I will keep careful watch upon this road towards the east .
